title: Low- and Mid-Tier Mobile for the Real World (2026)
excerpt: The 2026 update to my real-world device testing recommendations paints a clearer picture than ever…
content_html:
21 July, 2026

Written by Harry Roberts on CSS Wizardry.

View this page as Markdown.

Table of Contents

Independent writing is brought to you via my wonderful Supporters.

  1. What Makes a Representative Test Device?
  2. DevTools Is an Approximation, Not an Answer
  3. Real Low- and Mid-Tier Mobile Devices for 2026
    1. Samsung Again?
  4. Real Low-Tier Mobile for 2026: Samsung Galaxy A17 5G
    1. Why Not the Galaxy A16 5G?
    2. The Galaxy A07 4G Complication
  5. Real Mid-Tier Mobile for 2026: Samsung Galaxy A56 5G
    1. Why Not the Galaxy A57 5G?
  6. What About WebPageTest Equivalents?
  7. Closing Thoughts

Last year, I set out to find one representative low-tier and one representative mid-tier Android device for real-world web performance testing. Back then, I landed on the Samsung Galaxy A15 5G and Galaxy A54 5G. How much have things changed in a year?

Then, as it is now, the point wasn’t to find the slowest phones I could buy, nor was it to find two old flagships whose best days were behind them. Remember, low-tier does not mean old, and mid-tier does not mean a flagship from 2018. These are current device classes used by real people, with their own constraints around processing power, memory, storage, and price.

One year on, the method still holds up. Better yet, the market has given us much stronger evidence to work with. Let’s see what the best, real devices are for 2026…

tl;dr: If you want broadly representative Android hardware for real-world web performance testing in 2026, buy a Samsung Galaxy A17 5G (SM-A176x; 4 GB / 128 GB) for low-tier testing and a Samsung Galaxy A56 5G (SM-A566x; 8 GB) for mid-tier testing.

Both ranked among the world’s ten best-selling smartphones in Q1 2026, are widely ranged, receive long-term software support, and offer a meaningful separation in real browser performance1.

And, if a large proportion of your audience is in the Middle East, Africa, or Latin America, I would also consider a Galaxy A07 4G as a regional, entry-tier class of device.

What Makes a Representative Test Device?

My original criteria considered four factors:

  • Distribution and market penetration:
    • how likely is it that folk actually own the device?
  • Device and hardware capabilities:
    • does the hardware authentically represent its intended tier?
  • Support and longevity:
    • will the device remain relevant and secure for long enough to justify buying and maintaining it?
  • Price-point:
    • is this a realistic consumer purchase at that tier?

For 2026, I’m adding a fifth consideration:

  • Reproducibility and variant clarity:
    • can people in different markets buy comparable hardware, or does one model name conceal different internals?

This is to account for the fact that many manufacturers may vary device internals based on geographic locale and market, so a Galaxy A17 to you may well differ to a Galaxy A17 to me.

As ever, there is no single phone that can stand in for billions of Android devices. A broadly distributed default is useful in the general sense, but your own analytics should always get the final say. If most of your users are in one market, or you know that a particular handset dominates your traffic, test there too.

DevTools Is an Approximation, Not an Answer

Chrome DevTools’ calibrated CPU throttling is a useful starting point. It can approximate the CPU throughput of low- and mid-tier hardware relative to your development machine, while its network presets can constrain latency and bandwidth.

But DevTools now says this clearly: Throttling is relative to your computer’s capabilities […] DevTools can’t truly simulate the CPUs of mobile devices, because the architecture of mobile devices is very different from that of desktops and laptops2.

Nor can a slowdown multiplier reproduce a phone’s available memory, storage performance, thermal behaviour, scheduler, background processes, or power-management decisions, so, again, you’re working through a very crude proxy. In fact, in three notable projects since last year’s piece, I found myself using—and getting markedly different results on—a physical device than I do in DevTools.

Real Low- and Mid-Tier Mobile Devices for 2026

The Speedometer figures come from separate Notebookcheck reviews, not a controlled head-to-head test. The different Chrome versions alone make a direct comparison difficult. Still, the A56 scoring roughly 70% higher is useful corroboration that these two phones occupy meaningfully different performance tiers34.

Samsung Again?

At first glance, choosing two Samsung A-series phones for a second year running might look like lazy continuation, but I assure you, it isn’t!

I also considered current Redmi/Xiaomi, Motorola, Honor, OPPO/realme, Nothing/CMF, OnePlus, and Pixel devices. Several are excellent regional or price-specific alternatives—the Redmi A5 was the sole non-Samsung Android handset in Counterpoint’s Q1 2026 global top ten—but none combines model-level sales evidence, broad cross-regional availability, consistent hardware, long support, and the right performance constraints as neatly as these two.

Samsung’s grip on this particular part of the Android market is extraordinary: five of the six Android phones in that global top ten were Galaxy A-series devices1, while the A56 was Europe’s best-selling smartphone of 20255. The repetition is in a way a finding, and not just an assumption carried over from last year.

Real Low-Tier Mobile for 2026: Samsung Galaxy A17 5G

The Galaxy A17 5G is an unusually clean low-tier recommendation.

Samsung launched it in the UK in August 2025 at £199. The base configuration combines 4 GB RAM and 128 GB storage with an Exynos 1330 processor, a 6.7″ 90 Hz AMOLED display, six OS upgrades, and six years of security updates6. It subsequently launched nationwide in the US at $199.99, and official Samsung pages range the A17 5G in India, Australia, South Africa, and Singapore too78.

That gives us the right kind of ubiquity: not jsut a very cheap phone that sells heavily in one region, but a current low-tier device available across a useful spread of markets.

More importantly, people are actually buying it. The Galaxy A17 5G ranked fifth among all smartphones globally in Q1 2026, making it the highest-ranked 5G Android handset in Counterpoint’s table1.

The hardware gives us authentic constraints rather than novelty for novelty’s sake:

  • Release date: August 2025 in the UK
  • Cost at release: £199 UK; $199.99 US
  • Chipset: Exynos 1330
  • Recommended configuration: 4 GB RAM / 128 GB storage
  • Screen: 6.7″ Super AMOLED, 90 Hz
  • Support window: six OS upgrades and six years of security updates
  • Why it’s representative: low launch price, broad international ranging, long support, and direct global sales evidence

Notebookcheck’s tested 128 GB unit used eMMC storage and scored 7.07 runs per minute in Speedometer 3 under Chrome 140. The reviewer also found that browsing could feel slow at times3. That is useful real-world feedback: the A17 is not simply a desktop CPU with a larger slowdown multiplier. Slow storage, limited memory, and lower main-thread headroom all combine to be tangible.

One thing worth noting: I would not assume that every regional A17 5G has the exact same storage technology just because Notebookcheck’s handset used eMMC. Samsung’s configurations vary by market, so verify the exact model you are buying. For a deliberately constrained test device, the 4 GB / 128 GB variant is the one I’d go for.

Why Not the Galaxy A16 5G?

The A16 has had much longer to build an installed base and remains a perfectly relevant phone to test if you already own one. But there is a difference between asking what do users have? and what should I buy now?

For a new purchase in 2026, I’d favour the A17. It occupies the same £199 launch position, is currently ranged, has a longer useful life ahead of it, and had already reached fifth place globally within months of launch.

The Galaxy A07 4G Complication

There is one solid argument against the A17: the Galaxy A07 4G ranked fourth in Q1 2026, making it the world’s best-selling Android handset in Counterpoint’s table1.

Its sales contribution was particularly strong in the Middle East, Africa and Latin America. That makes the A07 arguably a more realistic floor for sites with a large audience in those regions.

Still, I would not replace the A17 with it as the single global default. The A07 4G is less far-ranged across the UK, Europe, and North America, while the A17 gives us a more reproducible cross-regional baseline. But this is precisely why one global recommendation will always be a compromise.

If your analytics lean heavily towards emerging markets, add the A07 4G as a regional entry-tier device below the A17. Your users are more important than broad averages, especially if they’re coming from emerging economies.

Real Mid-Tier Mobile for 2026: Samsung Galaxy A56 5G

The Galaxy A56 5G is an even easier recommendation.

Samsung launched the A56 in March 2025 with an Exynos 1580 processor, 8 GB RAM, a 6.7″ 120 Hz AMOLED display, and up to six generations of OS upgrades plus six years of security updates. The UK consumer model launched with 256 GB storage at £499; the US later received 128 GB and 256 GB variants from $499.99910.

Ordinarily, choosing a device released in the previous calendar year might look a little conservative or cautious. Here, however, it is the evidence-backed choice:

  • Omdia named the Galaxy A56 the top-selling smartphone model in Europe in 20255.
  • It then ranked seventh globally in Q1 20261.

That is about as direct an answer to how likely is it that folk actually have one of these? as we are going to get.

The hardware also provides a credible step above the A17 without drifting into flagship-lite territory:

  • Release date: March 2025
  • Cost at release: £499 UK for the consumer 8 GB / 256 GB model
  • Chipset: Exynos 1580
  • Memory: 8 GB
  • Storage: 128/256 GB; Notebookcheck’s tested 128 GB unit used UFS 3.1
  • Screen: 6.7″ Super AMOLED, up to 120 Hz
  • Support window: up to six OS/One UI generations and six years of security updates
  • Why it’s representative: mass-market reach, exceptional European sales evidence, global top-ten placement, and a material hardware step above the A17

Notebookcheck’s 128 GB test unit used UFS 3.1 storage and scored 12.0 runs per minute in Speedometer 3 under Chrome 1344. The faster processor, double the recommended RAM, and materially quicker storage give you a useful contrast with the A17—not just in raw benchmark throughput, but in cache access, memory pressure, and the amount of main-thread work the device can absorb before the experience deteriorates.

Why Not the Galaxy A57 5G?

Because newest is not the same as most representative.

The Galaxy A57 5G arrived in the UK on 10 April 2026, starting at £529 for the 8 GB / 256 GB model11. As of 21 July 2026, that gives it only a little over three months of market history. It may well become the obvious mid-tier recommendation in time, but it does not yet have the A56’s installed-base or sales evidence.

This is absence of evidence, not evidence that the A57 is unpopular. If authoritative sales data later shows it displacing the A56, the recommendation should change. But picking it today would reward catalogue freshness over the very thing we are trying to measure: the hardware people actually use.

The A56 is not the recommendation despite being one generation old. It is the recommendation because the market has already proved it representative.

What About WebPageTest Equivalents?

WebPageTest’s open-source configuration does list its emulated devices. Each preset is a small configuration of viewport width and height, device pixel ratio, user agent, and a throttle_cpu multiplier12. That gives us something a little more useful than inferring from the names shown in the device dropdown.

The closest presets I would use are:

The A17 sits between the Pixel 5 and Pixel 6 in general CPU benchmarks, but closer enough to the Pixel 5 to drop down a preset; the A56 is broadly Pixel 6/7-class. That is useful cross-referencing, but not a calibration: Geekbench is not a browser-based stress test, and WebPageTest’s slowdown is applied to its desktop test agent rather than modelling any real phone’s SoC13.

There is another limitation in the configuration itself. Pixel 6, Pixel 7, Galaxy S23, and every recent iPhone preset all use throttle_cpu=1: no additional slowdown at all. WebPageTest therefore collapses several materially different devices into the-performance-of-the-test-machine-they’re-running-on. Its agent can normalise CPU throttling across test machines, but it cannot make an unthrottled desktop behave like a particular phone14.

Further, the open source repository’s device file was last updated in December 2023, so names and values may change/have changed independently of this article. A physical A17 and A56 remain the references; the WebPageTest presets are convenient approximations when those phones are not available.

So, use these presets, but remember that these are the closest WebPageTest presets, not equivalent devices. (The network profile remains a separate choice and should be selected for the audience or demographic you’re testing against; it is not part of either device preset.)

Closing Thoughts

A representative test device is a solid reference point, but not always universal truth. The Android market is far too broad, regional, and fragmented for one phone to stand in for everyone.

But one well-chosen physical device is still vastly better than testing only on the powerful laptop on your desk and the flagship phone in your pocket. It exposes constraints that a slowdown multiplier cannot: memory pressure, slower storage, thermal behaviour, power management, and the point at which an interaction that felt fine on desktop turns into visible jank and annoyance.

For broadly representative Android testing in 2026, my choices are the Samsung Galaxy A17 5G (SM-A176x; 4 GB / 128 GB) for low-tier and the Samsung Galaxy A56 5G (SM-A566x; 8 GB) for mid-tier.

The A17 is current and already widely owned. The A56 proves why the newest model is not automatically the right one.

And if your audience is concentrated in emerging markets, the A07 4G is a useful reminder that the most honest baseline may be regional rather than global.

Happy testing!

  2. Chrome DevTools Performance reference: calibrated throttling can better match low- and mid-tier devices, but remains relative to the development computer and cannot truly simulate mobile CPU architecture. ↩

  3. Notebookcheck’s Galaxy A17 5G review: the tested 128 GB unit used eMMC storage and scored 7.07 in Speedometer 3 under Chrome 140. ↩ ↩2

  4. Notebookcheck’s Galaxy A56 5G review: the tested 128 GB unit used UFS 3.1 storage and scored 12.0 in Speedometer 3 under Chrome 134. ↩ ↩2

  5. Omdia’s European smartphone-market analysis for 2025: the Galaxy A56 was Europe’s top-selling smartphone model. ↩ ↩2

  6. Samsung UK’s Galaxy A17 5G launch announcement: £199 launch price, Exynos 1330, 4 GB / 128 GB base configuration, 90 Hz display, and six-year support policy. ↩

  7. Samsung US’s Galaxy A17 5G availability announcement: nationwide availability from 7 January 2026 at $199.99. ↩

  8. Official Samsung Galaxy A17 5G product pages for India, Australia, South Africa, and Singapore. Configurations differ by market. ↩

  9. Samsung UK’s Galaxy A56 5G launch announcement: Exynos 1580, 8 GB / 256 GB consumer configuration at £499, display, and update policy. ↩

  10. Samsung US’s Galaxy A56 5G availability announcement: nationwide availability, $499.99 starting price, and 128/256 GB variants. ↩

  11. Samsung UK’s Galaxy A57 5G availability announcement: available from 10 April 2026, starting at £529 for 8 GB / 256 GB. ↩

  12. WebPageTest’s open-source mobile_devices.ini: the current Pixel 5 preset uses a 393 × 722 viewport, DPR 2.75, and 2× CPU slowdown; Pixel 6 and Pixel 7 use 412-pixel-wide viewports, DPR 2.63, and no additional CPU slowdown. ↩

  13. Notebookcheck measured the Galaxy A17 5G at 988 single-core and 2,093 multi-core in Geekbench 6.7. Geekbench’s aggregated results place the Pixel 5 at 789/1,864, the Pixel 6 at 1,379/3,194, the Pixel 7 at 1,416/3,395, and the Galaxy A56 at 1,320/3,753. These figures establish only broad performance classes. ↩

  14. WebPageTest agent test options: throttle_cpu is a multiplier applied to Chrome and CPU normalisation adjusts it for the test machine. The configuration history shows its latest change on 12 December 2023. ↩

blocks.html_en: Last year, I set out to find one representative low-tier and one representative mid-tier Android device for real-world web performance testing. Back then, I landed on the Samsung Galaxy A15 5G and Galaxy A54 5G. How much have things changed in a year?

sentences.en: Last year, I set out to find one representative low-tier and one representative mid-tier Android device for real-world web performance testing.
sentences.zh: 去年，我开始寻找一款具有代表性的低端 Android 设备和一款具有代表性的中端 Android 设备来进行实际 Web 性能测试。

sentences.en: Back then, I landed on the Samsung Galaxy A15 5G and Galaxy A54 5G.
sentences.zh: 当时，我选择了三星 Galaxy A15 5G 和 Galaxy A54 5G。

sentences.en: How much have things changed in a year?
sentences.zh: 一年内发生了多少变化？

blocks.html_en: Then, as it is now, the point wasn’t to find the slowest phones I could buy, nor was it to find two old flagships whose best days were behind them. Remember, low-tier does not mean old, and mid-tier does not mean a flagship from 2018. These are current device classes used by real people, with their own constraints around processing power, memory, storage, and price.

sentences.en: Then, as it is now, the point wasn’t to find the slowest phones I could buy, nor was it to find two old flagships whose best days were behind them.

blocks.html_en: This is to account for the fact that many manufacturers may vary device internals based on geographic locale and market, so a Galaxy A17 to you may well differ to a Galaxy A17 to me.

blocks.html_en: As ever, there is no single phone that can stand in for billions of Android devices. A broadly distributed default is useful in the general sense, but your own analytics should always get the final say. If most of your users are in one market, or you know that a particular handset dominates your traffic, test there too.

blocks.html_en: DevTools Is an Approximation, Not an Answer

sentences.en: DevTools Is an Approximation, Not an Answer
sentences.zh: DevTools 是一个近似值，而不是答案

blocks.html_en: Chrome DevTools’ calibrated CPU throttling is a useful starting point. It can approximate the CPU throughput of low- and mid-tier hardware relative to your development machine, while its network presets can constrain latency and bandwidth.

sentences.en: Chrome DevTools’ calibrated CPU throttling is a useful starting point.
sentences.zh: Chrome DevTools 的校准 CPU 限制是一个有用的起点。

sentences.en: It can approximate the CPU throughput of low- and mid-tier hardware relative to your development machine, while its network presets can constrain latency and bandwidth.
sentences.zh: 它可以估算相对于您的开发机器的中低层硬件的 CPU 吞吐量，而其网络预设可以限制延迟和带宽。

blocks.html_en: But DevTools now says this clearly: Throttling is relative to your computer’s capabilities […] DevTools can’t truly simulate the CPUs of mobile devices, because the architecture of mobile devices is very different from that of desktops and laptops2.

sentences.en: But DevTools now says this clearly: Throttling is relative to your computer’s capabilities […]
sentences.zh: 但 DevTools 现在清楚地表明了这一点：节流与计算机的功能有关 [...]

sentences.en: DevTools can’t truly simulate the CPUs of mobile devices, because the architecture of mobile devices is very different from that of desktops and laptops2.
sentences.zh: DevTools 无法真正模拟移动设备的 CPU，因为移动设备的架构与台式机和笔记本电脑的架构有很大不同2。

blocks.html_en: Nor can a slowdown multiplier reproduce a phone’s available memory, storage performance, thermal behaviour, scheduler, background processes, or power-management decisions, so, again, you’re working through a very crude proxy. In fact, in three notable projects since last year’s piece, I found myself using—and getting markedly different results on—a physical device than I do in DevTools.

sentences.en: Nor can a slowdown multiplier reproduce a phone’s available memory, storage performance, thermal behaviour, scheduler, background processes, or power-management decisions, so, again, you’re working through a very crude proxy.
sentences.zh: 减速乘数也无法重现手机的可用内存、存储性能、热行为、调度程序、后台进程或电源管理决策，因此，您再次通过一个非常粗糙的代理进行工作。

sentences.en: In fact, in three notable projects since last year’s piece, I found myself using—and getting markedly different results on—a physical device than I do in DevTools.
sentences.zh: 事实上，自去年的文章以来，在三个值得注意的项目中，我发现自己在物理设备上使用的结果与在 DevTools 中使用的结果明显不同。

blocks.html_en: At first glance, choosing two Samsung A-series phones for a second year running might look like lazy continuation, but I assure you, it isn’t!

sentences.en: At first glance, choosing two Samsung A-series phones for a second year running might look like lazy continuation, but I assure you, it isn’t!

blocks.html_en: I also considered current Redmi/Xiaomi, Motorola, Honor, OPPO/realme, Nothing/CMF, OnePlus, and Pixel devices. Several are excellent regional or price-specific alternatives—the Redmi A5 was the sole non-Samsung Android handset in Counterpoint’s Q1 2026 global top ten—but none combines model-level sales evidence, broad cross-regional availability, consistent hardware, long support, and the right performance constraints as neatly as these two.

blocks.html_en: Samsung’s grip on this particular part of the Android market is extraordinary: five of the six Android phones in that global top ten were Galaxy A-series devices1, while the A56 was Europe’s best-selling smartphone of 20255. The repetition is in a way a finding, and not just an assumption carried over from last year.

sentences.zh: 这是有用的交叉引用，但不是校准：Geekbench 不是基于浏览器的压力测试，WebPageTest 的减速适用于其桌面测试代理，而不是对任何真实手机的 SoC13 进行建模。

blocks.html_en: There is another limitation in the configuration itself. Pixel 6, Pixel 7, Galaxy S23, and every recent iPhone preset all use throttle_cpu=1: no additional slowdown at all. WebPageTest therefore collapses several materially different devices into the-performance-of-the-test-machine-they’re-running-on. Its agent can normalise CPU throttling across test machines, but it cannot make an unthrottled desktop behave like a particular phone14.

sentences.en: There is another limitation in the configuration itself.
sentences.zh: 配置本身还有另一个限制。

sentences.en: Pixel 6, Pixel 7, Galaxy S23, and every recent iPhone preset all use throttle_cpu=1: no additional slowdown at all.
sentences.zh: Pixel 6、Pixel 7、Galaxy S23 和每个最新的 iPhone 预设都使用throttle_cpu=1：根本没有额外的减速。

sentences.en: WebPageTest therefore collapses several materially different devices into the-performance-of-the-test-machine-they’re-running-on.
sentences.zh: 因此，WebPageTest 将几个本质上不同的设备折叠到它们正在运行的测试机器的性能中。

sentences.en: Its agent can normalise CPU throttling across test machines, but it cannot make an unthrottled desktop behave like a particular phone14.
sentences.zh: 它的代理可以规范测试机器上的 CPU 节流，但它无法使未节流的桌面表现得像特定手机一样14。

blocks.html_en: Further, the open source repository’s device file was last updated in December 2023, so names and values may change/have changed independently of this article. A physical A17 and A56 remain the references; the WebPageTest presets are convenient approximations when those phones are not available.

sentences.en: Further, the open source repository’s device file was last updated in December 2023, so names and values may change/have changed independently of this article.
sentences.zh: 此外，开源存储库的设备文件上次更新于 2023 年 12 月，因此名称和值可能会独立于本文而更改/已经更改。

sentences.en: A physical A17 and A56 remain the references; the WebPageTest presets are convenient approximations when those phones are not available.
sentences.zh: 物理 A17 和 A56 仍然是参考；当这些手机不可用时，WebPageTest 预设是方便的近似值。

blocks.html_en: So, use these presets, but remember that these are the closest WebPageTest presets, not equivalent devices. (The network profile remains a separate choice and should be selected for the audience or demographic you’re testing against; it is not part of either device preset.)

sentences.en: So, use these presets, but remember that these are the closest WebPageTest presets, not equivalent devices.
sentences.zh: 因此，请使用这些预设，但请记住这些是最接近的 WebPageTest 预设，而不是等效的设备。

sentences.en: (The network profile remains a separate choice and should be selected for the audience or demographic you’re testing against; it is not part of either device preset.)
sentences.zh: （网络配置文件仍然是一个单独的选择，应根据您要测试的受众或人口统计进行选择；它不是任何设备预设的一部分。）

blocks.html_en: Closing Thoughts

sentences.en: Closing Thoughts
sentences.zh: 结束语

blocks.html_en: A representative test device is a solid reference point, but not always universal truth. The Android market is far too broad, regional, and fragmented for one phone to stand in for everyone.

sentences.en: A representative test device is a solid reference point, but not always universal truth.
sentences.zh: 具有代表性的测试设备是可靠的参考点，但并不总是普遍真理。

sentences.en: The Android market is far too broad, regional, and fragmented for one phone to stand in for everyone.
sentences.zh: Android 市场过于广阔、区域性和分散性，一款手机无法代表所有人。

blocks.html_en: But one well-chosen physical device is still vastly better than testing only on the powerful laptop on your desk and the flagship phone in your pocket. It exposes constraints that a slowdown multiplier cannot: memory pressure, slower storage, thermal behaviour, power management, and the point at which an interaction that felt fine on desktop turns into visible jank and annoyance.

sentences.en: But one well-chosen physical device is still vastly better than testing only on the powerful laptop on your desk and the flagship phone in your pocket.
sentences.zh: 但是，一台精心挑选的物理设备仍然比仅在办公桌上功能强大的笔记本电脑和口袋里的旗舰手机上进行测试要好得多。

sentences.en: It exposes constraints that a slowdown multiplier cannot: memory pressure, slower storage, thermal behaviour, power management, and the point at which an interaction that felt fine on desktop turns into visible jank and annoyance.
sentences.zh: 它暴露了减速乘数无法做到的限制：内存压力、存储速度较慢、热行为、电源管理，以及在桌面上感觉良好的交互变成明显的卡顿和烦恼的点。

blocks.html_en: For broadly representative Android testing in 2026, my choices are the Samsung Galaxy A17 5G (SM-A176x; 4 GB / 128 GB) for low-tier and the Samsung Galaxy A56 5G (SM-A566x; 8 GB) for mid-tier.

sentences.en: For broadly representative Android testing in 2026, my choices are the Samsung Galaxy A17 5G (SM-A176x; 4 GB / 128 GB) for low-tier and the Samsung Galaxy A56 5G (SM-A566x; 8 GB) for mid-tier.
sentences.zh: 对于 2026 年具有广泛代表性的 Android 测试，我的选择是针对低端设备的三星 Galaxy A17 5G（SM-A176x；4 GB / 128 GB）和针对中端设备的三星 Galaxy A56 5G（SM-A566x；8 GB）。

blocks.html_en: The A17 is current and already widely owned. The A56 proves why the newest model is not automatically the right one.

sentences.zh: A17 现已上市并已被广泛拥有。

blocks.html_en: And if your audience is concentrated in emerging markets, the A07 4G is a useful reminder that the most honest baseline may be regional rather than global.

sentences.zh: 如果您的受众集中在新兴市场，A07 4G 会提醒您，最诚实的基准可能是区域性的，而不是全球性的。

content_html_zh:
21 July, 2026

由 Harry Roberts 在 CSS Wizardry 上撰写。

以 Markdown 形式查看此页面。

Table of Contents

独立写作是通过我出色的支持者带给您的。

  1. 具有代表性的测试设备是什么？
  2. DevTools 是一个近似值，而不是答案
  3. 2026 年三星再次推出真正的中低端移动设备？
  4. 2026 年真正的低端手机：三星 Galaxy A17 5G 为什么不是 Galaxy A16 5G？ Galaxy A07 4G 复杂功能
  5. 2026 年真正的中端手机：三星 Galaxy A56 5G 为什么不是 Galaxy A57 5G？
  6. WebPageTest 等效项怎么样？
  7. 结束语

去年，我开始寻找一款具有代表性的低端 Android 设备和一款具有代表性的中端 Android 设备来进行实际 Web 性能测试。 当时，我选择了三星 Galaxy A15 5G 和 Galaxy A54 5G。 一年内发生了多少变化？

当时和现在一样，重点不是找到我能买到的最慢的手机，也不是找到两款最好的日子已经过去的老旗舰。 请记住，低端并不意味着旧，中端并不意味着 2018 年的旗舰。 这些是真实的人使用的当前设备类别，在处理能力、内存、存储和价格方面有自己的限制。

一年过去了，这个方法仍然有效。 更好的是，市场为我们提供了更有力的证据。 让我们看看 2026 年最好的、真实的设备是什么……

tl;dr：如果您想要在 2026 年使用具有广泛代表性的 Android 硬件进行实际网络性能测试，请购买三星 Galaxy A17 5G（SM-A176x；4 GB / 128 GB）进行低层测试，购买三星 Galaxy A56 5G（SM-A566x；8 GB）进行中层测试。

两者均跻身 2026 年第一季度全球十大最畅销智能手机之列，产品范围广泛，获得长期软件支持，并且在实际浏览器性能方面具有显着的差异1。

而且，如果您的大部分受众来自中东、非洲或拉丁美洲，我还会考虑将 Galaxy A07 4G 作为区域性入门级设备。

具有代表性的测试设备是什么？

我最初的标准考虑了四个因素：

  • 分销和市场渗透：人们实际拥有该设备的可能性有多大？
  • 设备和硬件功能：硬件是否真正代表其预期层？
  • 支持和寿命：设备是否能在足够长的时间内保持相关性和安全性，以证明购买和维护它的合理性？
  • 价格点：该级别的消费者购买是否现实？

对于 2026 年，我添加第五个考虑因素：

  • 可重复性和变体清晰度：不同市场的人们是否可以购买类似的硬件，或者一种型号名称是否隐藏了不同的内部结构？

这是因为许多制造商可能会根据地理位置和市场来改变设备内部结构，因此您眼中的 Galaxy A17 可能与我眼中的 Galaxy A17 很不同。

与以往一样，没有任何一款手机可以替代数十亿台 Android 设备。 广泛分布的默认值在一般意义上是有用的，但您自己的分析应该始终拥有最终决定权。 如果您的大多数用户都在一个市场，或者您知道一款特定的手机主导着您的流量，也可以在那里进行测试。

DevTools 是一个近似值，而不是答案

Chrome DevTools 的校准 CPU 限制是一个有用的起点。 它可以估算相对于您的开发机器的中低层硬件的 CPU 吞吐量，而其网络预设可以限制延迟和带宽。

但 DevTools 现在清楚地表明了这一点：节流与计算机的功能有关 [...] DevTools 无法真正模拟移动设备的 CPU，因为移动设备的架构与台式机和笔记本电脑的架构有很大不同2。

减速乘数也无法重现手机的可用内存、存储性能、热行为、调度程序、后台进程或电源管理决策，因此，您再次通过一个非常粗糙的代理进行工作。 事实上，自去年的文章以来，在三个值得注意的项目中，我发现自己在物理设备上使用的结果与在 DevTools 中使用的结果明显不同。

2026 年真正的低端和中端移动设备

车速表数据来自单独的 Notebookcheck 评论，而不是受控的面对面测试。 仅 Chrome 版本不同就很难进行直接比较。 尽管如此，A56 的得分高出大约 70% 是有用的证据，证明这两款手机占据了明显不同的性能等级34。

又是三星？

乍一看，连续第二年选择两款三星 A 系列手机可能看起来像是懒惰的延续，但我向你保证，事实并非如此！

我还考虑了当前的 Redmi/小米、摩托罗拉、Honor、OPPO/realme、Nothing/CMF、OnePlus 和 Pixel 设备。 有几个是优秀的区域或特定价格替代品——红米 A5 是 Counterpoint 2026 年第一季度全球前十名中唯一的非三星 Android 手机——但没有一个像这两款那样将型号级销售证据、广泛的跨区域可用性、一致的硬件、长期支持和正确的性能限制结合在一起。

三星对 Android 市场这一特定部分的控制力非同寻常：全球前十名的六款 Android 手机中有五款是 Galaxy A 系列设备1，而 A56 是 20255 年欧洲最畅销的智能手机。 从某种程度上来说，这种重复是一个发现，而不仅仅是去年的假设。

2026 年真正的低端手机：三星 Galaxy A17 5G

Galaxy A17 5G 是一款异常干净的低端推荐手机。

三星于 2025 年 8 月在英国推出，售价 199 英镑。 基本配置结合了 4 GB RAM 和 128 GB 存储以及 Exynos 1330 处理器、6.7 英寸 90 Hz AMOLED 显示屏、六项操作系统升级和六年安全更新6。 随后，它在美国全国上市，售价为 199.99 美元，三星官方页面也包括印度、澳大利亚、南非和新加坡的 A17 5G78。

这让我们拥有了一种适当的普遍性：不仅仅是一款在某个地区热销的非常便宜的手机，而是一款在广泛的市场上销售的当前低端设备。

更重要的是，人们确实在购买它。 2026 年第一季度，Galaxy A17 5G 在全球所有智能手机中排名第五，使其成为 Counterpoint 表 1 中排名最高的 5G Android 手机。

硬件给了我们真正的约束，而不是为了新奇而新奇：

  • 上映日期：2025 年 8 月在英国
  • 发行成本：英国 199 英镑； 199.99 美元
  • 芯片组：Exynos 1330
  • 推荐配置：4 GB RAM / 128 GB 存储
  • 屏幕：6.7英寸Super AMOLED，90 Hz
  • 支持窗口：六次操作系统升级和六年安全更新
  • 为何具有代表性：低发布价格、广泛的国际范围、长期支持和直接的全球销售证据

Notebookcheck 测试的 128 GB 设备使用 eMMC 存储，在 Chrome 140 下的 Speedometer 3 中得分为每分钟 7.07 次。 评论者还发现浏览有时会感觉很慢3。 这是有用的现实反馈：A17 不仅仅是一个具有更大减速倍数的桌面 CPU。 缓慢的存储、有限的内存和较低的主线程空间都结合起来是有形的。

值得注意的是：我不会仅仅因为 Notebookcheck 的手机使用 eMMC 就认为每个地区的 A17 5G 都具有完全相同的存储技术。 三星的配置因市场而异，因此请确认您购买的确切型号。 对于有意限制的测试设备，我会选择 4 GB / 128 GB 版本。

为什么不是 Galaxy A16 5G？

A16 花了更长的时间来建立安装基础，并且仍然是一款非常适合测试您是否已经拥有一部手机的手机。 但询问用户有什么是有区别的？ 我现在应该买什么？

对于 2026 年新购买的产品，我更喜欢 A17。 它占据了相同的 199 英镑的发射位置，目前处于范围内，具有更长的使用寿命，并且在发射几个月内就已经达到全球第五位。

Galaxy A07 4G 复杂功能

有一个反对 A17 的有力论据：Galaxy A07 4G 在 2026 年第一季度排名第四，使其成为 Counterpoint 表 1 中全球最畅销的 Android 手机。

其销售贡献在中东、非洲和拉丁美洲尤其强劲。 对于这些地区拥有大量观众的网站来说，A07 可以说是一个更现实的楼层。

尽管如此，我不会用它来取代 A17 作为单一的全球默认值。 A07 4G 在英国、欧洲和北美的范围较小，而 A17 为我们提供了更具可重复性的跨区域基线。 但这正是为什么一项全球建议始终是一种妥协的原因。

如果您的分析严重偏向新兴市场，请将 A07 4G 添加为 A17 以下的区域入门级设备。 您的用户比广泛的平均值更重要，特别是如果他们来自新兴经济体。

2026 年真正的中端手机：三星 Galaxy A56 5G

Galaxy A56 5G 是更容易推荐的。

三星于 2025 年 3 月推出了 A56，配备 Exynos 1580 处理器、8 GB RAM、6.7 英寸 120 Hz AMOLED 显示屏，以及多达六代操作系统升级和六年安全更新。 英国消费者型号推出 256 GB 存储，售价 499 英镑；美国随后收到了 128 GB 和 256 GB 版本，起价为 499.99910 美元。

通常，选择上一年发布的设备可能看起来有点保守或谨慎。 然而，这是有证据支持的选择：

  • Omdia 将 Galaxy A56 评为 20255 年欧洲最畅销的智能手机型号。
  • 随后，它在 20261 年第一季度排名全球第七。

这大约是对人们实际上拥有其中之一的可能性有多大的直接回答？ 正如我们将要得到的。

该硬件还提供了比 A17 更可靠的进步，而不会陷入旗舰级精简版领域：

  • 发布日期：2025 年 3 月
  • 发布成本：消费者 8 GB / 256 GB 型号为 499 英国英镑
  • 芯片组：Exynos 1580
  • 内存：8GB
  • 存储：128/256 GB； Notebookcheck 测试的 128 GB 设备使用 UFS 3.1
  • 屏幕：6.7英寸Super AMOLED，高达120 Hz
  • 支持窗口：最多六代操作系统/一个用户界面和六年的安全更新
  • 为何具有代表性：大众市场覆盖面、出色的欧洲销售证据、全球前十名的排名以及高于 A17 的硬件硬件

Notebookcheck 的 128 GB 测试单元使用 UFS 3.1 存储，在 Chrome 1344 下的 Speedometer 3 中得分为每分钟 12.0 次。 更快的处理器、双倍推荐的 RAM 以及更快的存储速度，让您可以与 A17 进行有用的对比——不仅在原始基准吞吐量方面，而且在缓存访问、内存压力以及设备在体验恶化之前可以吸收的主线程工作量方面。

为什么不是 Galaxy A57 5G？

因为最新的和最有代表性的不一样。

Galaxy A57 5G 于 2026 年 4 月 10 日抵达英国，8 GB / 256 GB 型号起价 529 英镑11。 截至 2026 年 7 月 21 日，它的市场历史只有三个月多一点。 它很可能及时成为明显的中端推荐产品，但它还没有 A56 的安装基础或销售证据。

这是缺乏证据，而不是A57不受欢迎的证据。 如果后来权威销售数据显示它取代了 A56，那么建议应该改变。 但今天选择它会奖励目录的新鲜度，而不是我们试图衡量的东西：人们实际使用的硬件。

尽管 A56 已经是一代了，但并不推荐。 之所以推荐，是因为市场已经证明其具有代表性。

WebPageTest 等效项怎么样？

WebPageTest 的开源配置确实列出了其模拟设备。 每个预设都是视口宽度和高度、设备像素比、用户代理和throttle_cpu multiplier12 的小型配置。 这为我们提供了比从设备下拉列表中显示的名称推断更有用的东西。

我会使用的最接近的预设是：

在一般 CPU 基准测试中，A17 位于 Pixel 5 和 Pixel 6 之间，但与 Pixel 5 足够接近，可以降低预设值； A56 大致属于 Pixel 6/7 级别。 这是有用的交叉引用，但不是校准：Geekbench 不是基于浏览器的压力测试，WebPageTest 的减速适用于其桌面测试代理，而不是对任何真实手机的 SoC13 进行建模。

配置本身还有另一个限制。 Pixel 6、Pixel 7、Galaxy S23 和每个最新的 iPhone 预设都使用throttle_cpu=1：根本没有额外的减速。 因此，WebPageTest 将几个本质上不同的设备折叠到它们正在运行的测试机器的性能中。 它的代理可以规范测试机器上的 CPU 节流，但它无法使未节流的桌面表现得像特定手机一样14。

此外，开源存储库的设备文件上次更新于 2023 年 12 月，因此名称和值可能会独立于本文而更改/已经更改。 物理 A17 和 A56 仍然是参考；当这些手机不可用时，WebPageTest 预设是方便的近似值。

因此，请使用这些预设，但请记住这些是最接近的 WebPageTest 预设，而不是等效的设备。 （网络配置文件仍然是一个单独的选择，应根据您要测试的受众或人口统计进行选择；它不是任何设备预设的一部分。）

结束语

具有代表性的测试设备是可靠的参考点，但并不总是普遍真理。 Android 市场过于广阔、区域性和分散性，一款手机无法代表所有人。

但是，一台精心挑选的物理设备仍然比仅在办公桌上功能强大的笔记本电脑和口袋里的旗舰手机上进行测试要好得多。 它暴露了减速乘数无法做到的限制：内存压力、存储速度较慢、热行为、电源管理，以及在桌面上感觉良好的交互变成明显的卡顿和烦恼的点。

对于 2026 年具有广泛代表性的 Android 测试，我的选择是针对低端设备的三星 Galaxy A17 5G（SM-A176x；4 GB / 128 GB）和针对中端设备的三星 Galaxy A56 5G（SM-A566x；8 GB）。

A17 现已上市并已被广泛拥有。 A56 证明了为什么最新型号并不一定就是正确的。

如果您的受众集中在新兴市场，A07 4G 会提醒您，最诚实的基准可能是区域性的，而不是全球性的。

测试愉快！
